## Summary

<!-- What does this PR do? One or two sentences. -->

## Related Issue

<!-- Link any issues this PR closes. Use `Fixes #123`, `Closes #123`,
     `Refs #123`, or `Depends on #123`. If unspecific, describe why
     the reviewers should care. -->

## Type of change

<!-- Check all that apply. -->

- [ ] feat (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [ ] fix (non-breaking change that fixes a bug)
- [ ] docs (documentation only)
- [ ] refactor (code change that neither fixes a bug nor adds a feature)
- [ ] chore (maintenance, tooling, or dependency changes)
- [ ] ci (CI, workflows, or automation changes)
- [ ] BREAKING CHANGE (existing functionality changes incompatibly — see below)

## Testing

<!-- How did you verify this works? Include the commands you ran, plus screenshots
     or screen recordings for UI changes. Be specific: "ran `pnpm test` locally"
     is better than "tested locally". -->

## Screenshots / recordings

<!-- Drag-and-drop images or paste a Loom URL. For UI work a before/after
     comparison is gold. -->

## Checklist

- [ ] CI passes (lint, typecheck, test, build, format:check)
- [ ] PR title follows Conventional Commits (`feat:`, `fix:`, `docs:`, etc.)
- [ ] Documentation updated (if needed)
- [ ] ADR added or updated (if this changes architecture)
- [ ] Migration is backward-compatible (or `BREAKING CHANGE` is declared below)
- [ ] Zod schema updated if API or form contracts changed
- [ ] No `any`, `ts-ignore`, or new secrets introduced

## Breaking Changes

<!-- If this PR introduces a breaking change, describe the consumer impact and
     what callers must do to migrate. Keep `BREAKING CHANGE` PRs small so
     they can be reviewed carefully. -->

## Documentation updated

<!-- If docs changed (README, ADRs, guides, API surface docs), list what
     was updated and why. -->
